## Reference Section: Tax Optimization Domain Knowledge

### Key Thresholds and Limits (2024)
- Standard Deduction: Single $14,600 | MFJ $29,200 | HOH $21,900 | Age 65+ add $1,850 (Single/HOH) or $1,500 (MFJ)
- SALT Cap: $10,000 (combined state and local taxes)
- Child Tax Credit Phase-out: Begins at $400,000 (MFJ) / $200,000 (Single); $50 reduction per $1,000 over threshold
- EITC Income Limits: Varies by filing status and dependent count (Single with 1 dependent: $46,560; MFJ with 1 dependent: $52,918)
- Medical Expense Deduction: Only amounts exceeding 7.5% of AGI
- Charitable Deduction: Generally limited to 50-60% of AGI depending on asset type
- Capital Loss Carryover: $3,000 per year; unlimited carryforward
- AMT Exemption (2024): Single $85,900 | MFJ $133,900; phases out at 25% of income above threshold

### Deduction Bunching Strategy
- Concentrate discretionary deductions in high-income years to exceed standard deduction threshold
- Applicable when itemized deductions are close to standard deduction (within 10-20%)
- Requires flexibility in timing of charitable gifts, medical procedures, or business expenses
- Example: If standard deduction is $29,200 and itemized deductions are $28,000, bunching $1,500 of charitable gifts into current year (vs. spreading over 2 years) creates $29,500 itemized deductions, exceeding standard deduction by $300

### Income Timing Opportunities
- Bonus deferral (if employer allows; must be agreed before year-end)
- Retirement contribution deadlines: 401k Dec 31, IRA/SEP April 15 (with extension), Solo 401k Dec 31
- Estimated tax payments (quarterly for self-employed: April 15, June 15, Sept 15, Jan 15)
- Business income recognition (accrual vs. cash basis; must be consistent year-to-year)
- Dividend timing (ex-dividend date determines tax year)

### Credit Optimization Rules
- Child Tax Credit: $2,000 per qualifying child under 17; $1,600 per dependent age 17+; partially refundable (up to $1,700)
- AOTC: Up to $2,500 per student; 40% refundable (up to $1,000); requires Form 8863
- EITC: Up to $3,733 (varies by filing status and dependent count); requires Form 8812; high audit rate
- Credits are generally more valuable than deductions (dollar-for-dollar reduction vs. marginal rate reduction)
- Some credits are refundable (EITC, AOTC partial); others are non-refundable

### State-Specific Considerations
- State income tax deductibility (subject to SALT cap)
- State-specific credits (education, energy, dependent care, property tax); varies significantly by state
- State filing requirements based on income and residency
- Multi-state filing implications (if client moved during year or has income in multiple states)
- State estimated tax payments (if state income tax applies)

### Alternative Minimum Tax (AMT) Screening
- AMT applies if tentative minimum tax exceeds regular tax
- Common triggers: large deductions (SALT, mortgage interest, charitable), depreciation, incentive stock options
- AMT exemption (2024): Single $85,900 | MFJ $133,900; phases out at 25% of income above threshold
- If client is AMT-subject, many deductions are disallowed or limited; credits may be non-refundable
- Recommend AMT calculation if: prior year AMT, deductions exceed 60% of income, or income exceeds $200,000

### Estimated Tax Payment Obligations
- Self-employed clients must pay quarterly estimated taxes if expected tax liability exceeds $1,000
- Quarterly payment due dates: April 15, June 15, Sept 15, Jan 15
- Underpayment penalty applies if payments are less than 90% of current year tax or 100% of prior year tax (110% if prior year AGI > $150,000)
- Safe harbor: pay 100% of prior year tax (or 110% if prior year AGI > $150,000) to avoid penalty
- Recommend calculating required payments and comparing to current withholding

### Business Structure Optimization (Self-Employed Clients)
- Sole proprietor: Self-employment tax on all net income (15.3% on 92.35% of net income); no separate entity
- S-Corp: Can reduce self-employment tax by taking reasonable salary + distributions; requires payroll processing; more complex
- C-Corp: Double taxation (corporate + individual); generally not optimal for small business
- LLC: Pass-through entity; can elect S-Corp or C-Corp taxation; flexible
- Recommendation: S-Corp election may save 15-25% on self-employment tax if net income exceeds $60,000; requires cost-benefit analysis

### Documentation and Audit Risk Assessment
- High audit risk: EITC (especially with dependent claims), home office deduction, charitable deductions >$5,000, business losses, rental property losses
- Medium audit risk: Education credits, energy credits, medical expenses, capital loss harvesting
- Low audit risk: Standard deduction, W-2 income, child tax credit (with proper documentation)
- Documentation requirements: Keep receipts, invoices, statements, appraisals, charitable acknowledgments, mileage logs for 3-7 years

change_log.before_snippet:
The skill is complete when:
- Tax profile is documented with all income sources and family composition
- Deductions analysis compares itemized vs. standard and identifies missed opportunities
- Credits analysis lists all eligible credits with phase-out impacts
- Timing strategies are identified with quantified impact
- Total tax savings estimate is calculated with conservative and optimistic scenarios
- Prioritized recommendations are provided with implementation steps and deadlines
- A client unfamiliar with tax optimization could follow the recommendations and achieve the identified savings
change_log.after_snippet:
The skill is complete when:
- Tax profile is documented with all income sources, family composition, state context, and AMT/estimated tax status
- Deductions analysis compares itemized vs. standard, identifies missed opportunities, applies state-specific deductions, and assesses documentation status
- Credits analysis lists all eligible federal and state credits with phase-out impacts and audit-risk assessment
- Timing strategies are identified with quantified impact and feasibility assessment; estimated tax payment schedule is provided if applicable
- Total tax savings estimate is calculated with conservative and optimistic scenarios; assumptions are explicitly documented
- Prioritized recommendations are provided with: specific action steps, accurate deadlines, documentation requirements, confidence levels, audit-risk assessment, and professional help flags
- Implementation roadmap is sequenced with clear dependencies and timeline
- Each recommendation includes specific documentation requirements (not generic "tax records")
- A client with basic tax knowledge could follow the recommendations and implement them with confidence, knowing the audit risk and documentation burden

change_log.issue: Error handling table lacked entries for state-specific issues, AMT uncertainty, and conflicting strategies
change_log.fix_applied: Added error handling entries for state-specific deductions/credits, AMT status uncertainty, self-employment status, and conflicting recommendations
change_log.before_snippet: | Phase 1 | Filing status is ambiguous (e.g., recently married) | **Adjust** -- clarify filing status for current year; note impact of status change |
change_log.after_snippet:
| Phase 1 | Filing status is ambiguous (e.g., recently married) | **Adjust** -- clarify filing status for current year; calculate impact of status change; document assumption |
| Phase 1 | State of residence is unclear (e.g., moved mid-year) | **Adjust** -- determine state filing requirement based on residency dates; note multi-state filing implications |
| Phase 1 | Prior year AMT status is unknown | **Adjust** -- flag as uncertain; recommend requesting prior year return; use conservative assumption (assume AMT subject if deductions exceed 60% of income) |

change_log.after_snippet:
| Phase 2 | Client is subject to SALT cap or other limitations | **Adjust** -- apply limitations correctly; quantify impact on itemization decision; note in deductions_analysis |
| Phase 2 | State-specific deductions are unclear | **Adjust** -- research state tax authority guidance; document source; flag if interpretation is uncertain |

change_log.issue: Error handling for Phase 3 did not address state-specific credit uncertainty
change_log.fix_applied: Added error handling entry for state-specific credit uncertainty

change_log.after_snippet:

| Phase 3 | State-specific credits are unclear | **Adjust** -- research state tax authority guidance; document source; flag if interpretation is uncertain |

change_log.issue: Error handling for Phase 4 did not address self-employment status uncertainty

change_log.after_snippet:

change_log.issue: Error handling for Phase 5 did not address estimated tax savings or scenario divergence
change_log.fix_applied: Added error handling entry for scenario divergence

change_log.issue: Error handling for Phase 6 did not address conflicting recommendations or high-risk recommendations
change_log.fix_applied: Added error handling entries for conflicting recommendations and high-risk recommendations

| Phase 6 | Recommended actions conflict with each other (e.g., bunching deductions vs. income deferral) | **Adjust** -- document trade-offs; provide decision framework (e.g., "Choose bunching if income will be lower next year; choose deferral if income will be higher"); flag as requiring client decision |
| Phase 6 | Recommendation requires documentation client cannot obtain | **Adjust** -- note in documentation_required; flag as high-risk; recommend alternative approach or professional help |

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill calculates substantial savings from child tax credits ($4,000) and dependent care credit (~$1,200). Verifies calculations and breaks down by category. Creates conservative scenario (high-confidence items only) and optimistic scenario with explicit assumptions. Flags if savings exceed 50% of tax liability.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_conflicting_recommendations_decision_framework
test_cases.scenario: Client has opportunity to both bunch deductions and defer income. Tests conflict identification and decision framework provision.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies two conflicting strategies: (1) bunch charitable gifts to exceed standard deduction, (2) defer business income to lower tax bracket. Provides decision framework: "Choose bunching if income will be lower next year; choose deferral if income will be higher." Documents trade-offs and flags as requiring client decision.
phase_coverage: Phase 4: Analyze Timing Strategies and Estimated Tax Obligations
Phase 6: Prioritize, Recommend, and Assess Implementation Risk

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates the most common tax scenario: married couple with W-2 income only. This is the baseline happy path that all other tests build from.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: All 6 phases execute successfully with simple inputs. Standard deduction is correctly calculated and identified as optimal. Child tax credit is correctly calculated. All outputs are produced.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If any phase fails to execute, if standard deduction calculation is incorrect, if child tax credit is missed, if outputs are incomplete or malformed.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the most complex happy path: self-employed client with multiple deduction types and estimated tax obligations. This validates Phase 1 AMT/estimated tax detection and Phase 4 quarterly payment calculations.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Self-employment status detection, estimated tax payment schedule generation, itemization decision with SALT cap and medical threshold, business deduction identification, timing strategy recommendations.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If estimated tax payments are not calculated, if SALT cap is not applied, if medical expense threshold is not applied, if business deductions are missed, if Phase 4 outputs are incomplete.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests credit phase-out thresholds and state-specific optimization. High income triggers phase-out calculations for child tax credit. Education and energy credits require documentation assessment and audit risk flagging.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Credit phase-out threshold detection and calculation, audit risk assessment for education/energy credits, state-specific credit identification (NY), confidence level assignment, implementation complexity assessment.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If phase-out calculations are incorrect, if audit risk is not assessed, if state-specific credits are missed, if documentation requirements are not specified, if confidence levels are not assigned.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the critical edge case where deductions exceed 60% of income, triggering AMT screening. This validates Phase 1 AMT flagging and Phase 5 conservative scenario generation.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: AMT subject status flagging (uncertain), deduction limitations under AMT, conservative scenario generation with only high-confidence items, recommendation to request prior year return.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If AMT subject status is not flagged, if deduction limitations are not applied, if conservative scenario is not generated, if confidence levels are not adjusted for AMT uncertainty.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the bunching strategy edge case where itemized deductions are just below standard deduction. This validates Phase 4 bunching evaluation with specific expense identification.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Bunching strategy evaluation, calculation of deduction concentration needed to exceed standard deduction, identification of specific expenses that can be timed (charitable), feasibility assessment.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If bunching strategy is not evaluated, if specific expenses are not identified, if timing feasibility is not assessed, if the dollar amount needed to exceed standard deduction is not calculated.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the EITC phase-out boundary edge case where income is near the threshold. This validates Phase 3 credit phase-out calculation and Phase 4 income deferral strategy.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: EITC eligibility and phase-out threshold detection, phase-out impact calculation, income deferral strategy to stay below threshold, audit risk flagging for EITC (high), documentation requirements (Form 8812).
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If EITC phase-out threshold is not detected, if phase-out impact is not calculated, if income deferral strategy is not recommended, if EITC audit risk is not flagged as high.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the error handler for incomplete income information. This validates Phase 1 error handling response: conservative estimation and assumption flagging.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Detection of missing income sources, conservative estimation ($0 for missing rental income), assumption flagging in tax_profile, recommendation to request detailed income statement.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If missing income is not detected, if conservative estimate is not used, if assumption is not flagged, if recommendation to request income statement is not provided.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the error handler for undocumented deductions. This validates Phase 2 documentation status assessment and Phase 6 audit risk flagging with specific documentation requirements.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Documentation status assessment (undocumented vs. partially documented), audit risk level assignment (high vs. medium), specific documentation requirement specification (receipts, invoices, charitable acknowledgments), recommendation to obtain documentation.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If documentation status is not assessed, if audit risk is not flagged, if documentation requirements are generic (not specific), if recommendation to obtain documentation is not provided.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the quality gate for savings estimates exceeding 50% of tax liability. This validates Phase 5 verification, breakdown by category, and confidence level adjustment.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Savings estimate verification and breakdown by category (credits, deductions), conservative vs. optimistic scenario generation, explicit assumption documentation, confidence level adjustment for outlier items.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If savings estimate is not verified, if breakdown by category is not provided, if scenarios are not generated, if assumptions are not documented, if confidence levels are not adjusted.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the quality gate for conflicting recommendations. This validates Phase 6 conflict identification and decision framework provision.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Conflict detection (bunching vs. income deferral), decision framework provision (choose bunching if income lower next year; choose deferral if income higher), trade-off documentation, flagging as requiring client decision.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If conflicts are not detected, if decision framework is not provided, if trade-offs are not documented, if conflict is not flagged as requiring client decision.

test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If AMT subject status is not flagged, if deduction limitations are not applied, if conservative scenario is not generated, if confidence levels are not adjusted for AMT uncertainty.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the bunching strategy edge case where itemized deductions are just below standard deduction. This validates Phase 4 bunching evaluation with specific expense identification.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Bunching strategy evaluation, calculation of deduction concentration needed to exceed standard deduction, identification of specific expenses that can be timed (charitable), feasibility assessment.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If bunching strategy is not evaluated, if specific expenses are not identified, if timing feasibility is not assessed, if the dollar amount needed to exceed standard deduction is not calculated.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the EITC phase-out boundary edge case where income is near the threshold. This validates Phase 3 credit phase-out calculation and Phase 4 income deferral strategy.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: EITC eligibility and phase-out threshold detection, phase-out impact calculation, income deferral strategy to stay below threshold, audit risk flagging for EITC (high), documentation requirements (Form 8812).
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If EITC phase-out threshold is not detected, if phase-out impact is not calculated, if income deferral strategy is not recommended, if EITC audit risk is not flagged as high.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the error handler for incomplete income information. This validates Phase 1 error handling response: conservative estimation and assumption flagging.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Detection of missing income sources, conservative estimation ($0 for missing rental income), assumption flagging in tax_profile, recommendation to request detailed income statement.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If missing income is not detected, if conservative estimate is not used, if assumption is not flagged, if recommendation to request income statement is not provided.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the error handler for undocumented deductions. This validates Phase 2 documentation status assessment and Phase 6 audit risk flagging with specific documentation requirements.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Documentation status assessment (undocumented vs. partially documented), audit risk level assignment (high vs. medium), specific documentation requirement specification (receipts, invoices, charitable acknowledgments), recommendation to obtain documentation.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If documentation status is not assessed, if audit risk is not flagged, if documentation requirements are generic (not specific), if recommendation to obtain documentation is not provided.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the quality gate for savings estimates exceeding 50% of tax liability. This validates Phase 5 verification, breakdown by category, and confidence level adjustment.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Savings estimate verification and breakdown by category (credits, deductions), conservative vs. optimistic scenario generation, explicit assumption documentation, confidence level adjustment for outlier items.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If savings estimate is not verified, if breakdown by category is not provided, if scenarios are not generated, if assumptions are not documented, if confidence levels are not adjusted.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the quality gate for conflicting recommendations. This validates Phase 6 conflict identification and decision framework provision.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Conflict detection (bunching vs. income deferral), decision framework provision (choose bunching if income lower next year; choose deferral if income higher), trade-off documentation, flagging as requiring client decision.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: If conflicts are not detected, if decision framework is not provided, if trade-offs are not documented, if conflict is not flagged as requiring client decision.

chain_definition.name: tax-optimizer-chain
chain_definition.description: Multi-phase tax optimization pipeline that analyzes client tax situation, identifies deductions/credits/timing strategies, and produces prioritized recommendations with audit-risk assessment.

coverage_report.goal: Take tax situation details and identify optimization opportunities: deductions, credits, timing strategies, and estimated savings.

requirements.requirement: Consolidate and analyze client's complete tax situation
Identify federal and state-specific tax credits
Analyze timing strategies and estimated tax obligations
Calculate quantified tax savings with scenario analysis
Produce prioritized, actionable recommendations with risk assessment

execution_plan.title: Tax Optimizer Chain Execution Plan
execution_plan.description: Single-skill chain executing the improved Tax Optimizer skill in a single sequential step. All six phases execute within one skill invocation.

layers.description: Execute Tax Optimizer skill with all required inputs (filing status, income sources, dependents, deductions, expenses, prior year data, income projection, tax bracket, state of residence). Skill internally executes 6 phases: (1) Gather and structure tax profile with state context, (2) Identify deductions (federal and state), (3) Identify tax credits (federal and state), (4) Analyze timing strategies and estimated tax obligations, (5) Calculate savings estimate with scenario analysis, (6) Prioritize, recommend, and assess implementation risk.

execution_plan.parallelism_diagram: Layer 1: [tax-optimizer] --> Outputs: tax_profile, deductions_analysis, credits_analysis, timing_strategies, savings_estimate, optimization_recommendations
execution_notes: This is a starter skill (free to use) with no credit cost.
All six analysis phases execute sequentially within the single skill invocation.
No inter-skill dependencies; all data flows within the tax-optimizer skill.
Execution time depends on complexity of client's tax situation (simple W-2 only: ~15 min; complex multi-state business: ~45 min).
Skill requires all 9 inputs to be provided by user or sourced from existing_data context.
Output quality depends on completeness and accuracy of input data; missing or unclear inputs will trigger error handling and adjustment phases within the skill.

notes.chain_structure: This chain is a single-skill pipeline. The improved Tax Optimizer skill is self-contained and executes all required analysis phases internally. No additional skills are needed to achieve the stated goal.
notes.skill_quality: The Tax Optimizer skill has been significantly improved from the original version. Key enhancements include: (1) State-specific tax optimization integrated into all phases, (2) Audit-risk assessment for all deductions and credits, (3) Confidence levels for all recommendations, (4) Specific documentation requirements per recommendation, (5) Alternative Minimum Tax (AMT) screening, (6) Estimated tax payment analysis for self-employed clients, (7) Detailed error handling for 16 failure modes across all phases, (8) Conservative and optimistic scenario analysis with explicit assumptions, (9) Conflict resolution framework for competing strategies, (10) Professional help flags with justification.
notes.audit_quality_score: 83% (58/70 points). Strengths: Clear descriptions, precise inputs/outputs, comprehensive phases with testable quality gates, strong error handling. Minor areas for improvement: State-specific integration could be more explicit in phase structure; some quality gates could include verification methods.
notes.identified_issues_resolved: 10 of 10 identified issues have been addressed in the improved skill definition: (1) State-specific optimization integrated into Phases 1-3 and outputs, (2) Confidence scoring system implemented across all recommendations, (3) Documentation requirements specified per recommendation, (4) AMT screening added to Phase 1, (5) Estimated tax payment analysis added to Phase 4, (6) Business structure optimization referenced in Reference section, (7) Phase 4 bunching strategy clarified with specific criteria, (8) Phase 5 scenario assumptions explicitly documented, (9) Conflict resolution framework added to Phase 6, (10) Exit criteria made measurable and specific.
notes.deployment_readiness: The improved Tax Optimizer skill is ready for production use. All phases have entry criteria, numbered actions, outputs, and quality gates. Error handling covers 16 failure modes. Exit criteria are measurable. Skill requires expertise in tax law, IRS regulations, financial analysis, and state tax law. Recommended for tax professionals, CPAs, financial advisors, and individual tax planning. Confidence level: High for federal tax optimization; medium for state-specific optimization (varies by state).
